hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card pins the current failure magnitude from the authoritative `/work/xr` gap artifact, so stale control-plane ratchets cannot target vague XR severity.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `sysopt.xr-*` cards bound the authoritative gap and first broken law but not the current severity baseline, so a stale control-plane slice could still speak about XR failure magnitude vaguely after the gap changed. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects any XR card whose `severity_baseline` is missing or drifts from `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json.severity_counts`, and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails XR cards with missing or stale `severity_baseline` counts while the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR systems-optimizer ratchets must pin the current severity baseline from the authoritative gap artifact, or route-neutral work can optimize against stale failure magnitude.
